description:
Advances: mutagen.mutation_pipeline, mutagen.json_schema

Deliverable:
- Fixture project at `test/fixtures/lane_project/` with its own `mix.exs`
  and the module set named by the testability red team:
  `lib/lane_fixture/{arith,decisions,guarded,pipelined,result_tuples,
  macro_holder,struct_holder,no_defs,infinite_looper,ecto_user}.ex`.
- Per-module test files at `test/fixtures/lane_project/test/lane_fixture/`
  (one "tight" and one "toothless" test per module, plus
  `red_baseline_test.exs`).
- `test/mutagen_ex/end_to_end_test.exs` invokes the pipeline via
  `Mix.Task.rerun("mutagen", [...])` against the lane_project for each spec
  scenario (1-4) plus baseline-red and zero-coverage-zero-mutation.
- Output JSON diffed against the golden fixtures from S6.

Verification:
- Tests to pass: `mix test --include integration test/mutagen_ex/end_to_end_test.exs`.
- All four "simple" mutator categories (arithmetic, comparison, boolean,
  literal) produce mutations on `arith.ex` and `decisions.ex`.
- The six "Elixir-flavored" mutators (`with`-swap, `case`-drop, pipeline-flip,
  result-tuple-flip, `else` removal, guard-drop) produce mutations on their
  respective modules and EITHER kill cleanly OR record as `:skipped` with
  reason — never `:compile_error` flakes.
- `infinite_looper.ex` mutation classifies as `:timeout`.
- `ecto_user.ex` (the C1 fixture analogue) survives a full mutation pass
  with bytecode-identical restore (Spike I invariants re-run as part of
  this test file).
- Covers spec-verification stubs covered indirectly: `mutagen.mutation_pipeline.v1`
  through `.v4`, `mutagen.json_schema.v3` (goldens).

Out of Scope (files):
- `lib/mutagen_ex/**` (this stage is fixture + end-to-end only)
- `test/mutagen_ex/integration/c1_test.exs`, `c2_test.exs` (S2; this stage
  may RE-RUN them but does not modify them)
- `lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex` (S6)

Allowed touches (this stage may modify):
- `test/fixtures/lane_project/**`
- `test/mutagen_ex/end_to_end_test.exs`

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do not silently weaken the lane fixture to make tests pass. If a scenario
  fails on a real implementation gap, that's a discovery — file a separate
  ticket and fix in `lib/`, not by editing the fixture.
- `infinite_looper.ex`'s deterministic infinite-loop mutation is a `case`
  clause drop on a recursive function's base case (per the refined plan's
  S7 risk note). Do not pick a non-deterministic trigger.

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly with `--warnings-as-errors`
- [ ] End-to-end suite passes
- [ ] Each scenario maps to a golden fixture and matches byte-for-byte
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket
title: S7: Lane fixture + end-to-end integration tests
